query: We're on Node 18 and our package.json has express 4.18.2, helmet 7.0.0, and jsonwebtoken 9.0.0. I want to upgrade everything to latest before we ship next sprint. Can you check what's current and whether anything will break our auth middleware?

I need to set up a GitHub Actions workflow for our monorepo. It should run eslint, vitest, and build Docker images for 3 services. We're using pnpm workspaces. Make sure the actions are pinned properly — we got burned by the tj-actions thing last year.
hey can you look at our terraform/main.tf and check if the aws provider version and the module versions are still current? we havent touched infra in like 8 months and im worried stuff has drifted

We need to migrate from CircleCI to GitHub Actions. Current config is in .circleci/config.yml — it runs pytest, mypy, black, and deploys to AWS ECS via a custom orb. Can you plan out what the equivalent GHA workflow would look like?

Our Dockerfile uses python:3.11-slim as base and installs poetry 1.6.1. I think both might be behind. Also the multi-stage build copies from node:18-alpine for the frontend. Can you verify all the base images and tool versions are current and flag any CVEs?

I'm reviewing this PR that updates our Helm chart values — it bumps the ingress-nginx controller to 1.9.4 and cert-manager to 1.13.2. Are those actually the latest? Last time someone submitted outdated versions that had a known vuln.

we have a pre-commit config with ruff, mypy, black, and bandit. havent updated the revs in forever. also i think black might be deprecated now in favor of ruff format? can you check whats going on and tell me what to do

I wrote a CloudFormation template that creates an EKS cluster with managed node groups. Before I deploy it to prod, can you review the template and make sure the AMI types, Kubernetes version, and add-on versions are all current? I don't want to deploy something that's already EOL.

Planning to add Dependabot to the repo. I need a .github/dependabot.yml that covers npm, pip, GitHub Actions, and Docker. What are the current best practices for the config? I've heard the update schedule options changed recently.

Our pyproject.toml pins SQLAlchemy==2.0.23, alembic==1.12.1, and pydantic==2.5.2. We're about to start a new feature that needs the JSON column type improvements. Can you check if we need to upgrade and whether our existing models will still work?

Can you refactor the UserService class to use dependency injection instead of importing the repository directly? The class is in src/services/user_service.py and it's getting hard to test because of all the concrete dependencies.

I need to write a database migration that adds a status column to the orders table with a default value of 'pending' and backfills existing rows. We're using Alembic. Can you generate the migration script?
Explain how our authentication middleware works. It's in src/middleware/auth.ts and I think it validates JWTs but I'm not sure about the refresh token flow. Walk me through it.
Write unit tests for the calculate_shipping_cost function in logistics/shipping.py. It takes weight, dimensions, and destination zone. Cover edge cases like zero weight and oversized packages.

The CI pipeline is failing on the lint step — getting 'no matching version found for eslint-config-airbnb@latest'. Can you figure out what's wrong and fix it? The error started after someone merged a PR yesterday.

I need to design the REST API for our new notifications service. It should support email, SMS, and push notifications with templating. Can you sketch out the endpoints, request/response schemas, and suggest a queue-based architecture?

We're switching from our custom branching model to trunk-based development. Can you update the CONTRIBUTING.md to document the new workflow — short-lived feature branches, squash merges, release tags from main?
Our GitHub Actions workflow takes 18 minutes. Can you look at .github/workflows/ci.yml and find ways to speed it up? Maybe we can parallelize the test matrix or improve caching.
I need to set up proper error handling in our Express app. Right now uncaught exceptions crash the process. Add a global error handler middleware and make sure async route errors are caught.
Can you create a git hook that runs our formatting and lint checks before each commit? I want it in a .husky/ directory so the whole team gets it automatically when they install deps.
I want to create a script that connects to the Nautobot API using the latest OpenAPI schema.
